codeReview.summary: The commit cleanly implements the requested desktop navbar polish: animated pill removed, active/inactive tab colors updated, and the header background made opaque without blur. Existing nav behaviors (tabs, search, auth controls, wallet/mobile menu flow) remain intact in the touched code path.

issues.description: Mobile menu tab styling still uses a filled active background (`bg-secondary`) and legacy inactive color (`text-muted-foreground`) rather than color-only active/inactive treatment. Desktop behavior matches the feature intent, but mobile tab-state styling is not aligned.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill procedure requires both typecheck and lint, but this run only executed typecheck while still reporting `followedProcedure: true` in handoff skillFeedback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md step 6 requires `cd web && pnpm typecheck` and `cd web && pnpm lint`; handoff verification.commandsRun includes only `pnpm -r typecheck`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The configured web healthcheck may be brittle for dev-server readiness and can report false negatives, causing unnecessary troubleshooting/restarts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml web.healthcheck is `curl -sf http://localhost:3102`; transcript shows repeated 'Web service is NOT running' while subsequent checks returned HTTP 404 and browser navigation intermittently succeeded.

summary: Review passed. Feature requirements are met for the main navbar path with no blocking correctness issues; one non-blocking mobile styling inconsistency and two shared-state/process observations were recorded.
